title: Widely-discussed public target: ~100,000 new dwellings per year
exactPromiseText: A ~100,000 new dwellings per year headline target has been used in Dutch housing policy since the Hugo de Jonge era. Its exact wording and enforcement mechanism differ across coalition programmes; the number itself is the shared reference point.
owner: Recent Dutch coalition programmes (specific attribution demo — verification pending)

source.document: Cabinet housing programme (specific document reference pending verification)

notes: The 100k/year target is genuinely referenced across Dutch housing policy. What we have NOT independently verified for this build: the specific cabinet name owning the current version, the URL of the current coalition programme, and the exact verbatim wording of the current commitment. CBS 82235NED delivers the actual measurement (79,910 in 2025).

title: Legal framework: central direction of provincial housing targets
exactPromiseText: Dutch housing legislation (naming and specific enactment date pending verification) gives the responsible minister authority to set provincial housing targets and designate local build sites, turning the headline 100k/year figure into an enforceable instrument.
owner: Dutch statutory framework (specific act pending verification)

source.document: Wet regie volkshuisvesting (identifier pending verification against wetten.overheid.nl)

notes: A statute of this name has been publicly discussed. Its exact wetten.overheid.nl identifier and current enacted status have not been re-verified in this build. Once verified, the demo flag drops and we cite the confirmed BWBR ID and enactment date.
